conclusions.calibrationDiscountReason: This domain has limited resolved calibration history; headline confidence is discounted per the scoreboard.

payload.conclusionText: Institutions encode information—not only incentives—and reform should be evaluated on epistemic grounds as well as efficiency.
payload.rationale: Synthesized from internal deliberation; illustrative public bundle row.
payload.evidenceSummary: Cross-episode claim chains link incentive stories to error-correction structures; adversarial challenges on reductionism were engaged and answered with explicit mechanisms.
exitConditions: A large natural experiment shows institution design has no measurable effect on collective belief accuracy under preregistered criteria.
A better explanatory model fits the same episodes without an information-encoding channel.

strongestObjection.objection: Incentive compatibility might fully explain observed institutional persistence; the 'information' channel may be redundant.
strongestObjection.firmAnswer: We treat redundancy as an empirical question: the model stays until a tighter incentive-only account predicts the same fine-grained update patterns across cases.

openQuestionsAdjacent: How should the firm score 'belief accuracy' outcomes in messy real institutions?

voiceComparisons.stance: Converges on dispersed knowledge; diverges on how much formal design can improve epistemic routing.

whatWouldChangeOurMind: A large natural experiment shows institution design has no measurable effect on collective belief accuracy under preregistered criteria.
A better explanatory model fits the same episodes without an information-encoding channel.

exitConditions: A large natural experiment shows institution design has no measurable effect on collective belief accuracy under preregistered criteria.

strongestObjection.objection: The 'information encoding' claim may collapse into a relabeling of incentive structures.
strongestObjection.firmAnswer: We operationalize 'encoding' as constraints on belief updates that are not reducible to private payoff shifts; v2 cites the concrete update tests used.

conclusions.calibrationDiscountReason: Small discount: stated confidence already modest and domain calibration is thin but not hostile.

payload.conclusionText: For this firm, methodological discipline is itself the product: public standing should track demonstrated process, not episodic visibility.
payload.rationale: Illustrative bundle row for the public site proof.
payload.evidenceSummary: Internal scoreboard + publication policy commitments reduce overconfidence incentives; adversarial engagement is treated as a gate, not theater.
exitConditions: A sustained period of public predictions shows calibration materially worse than the internal ledger without corrective updates.
External audit finds systematic mismatch between published claims and internal evidence trails.

strongestObjection.objection: Process obsession can crowd out object-level truth seeking.
strongestObjection.firmAnswer: Process here is instrumental: it is judged by whether it increases the rate of correct updates on falsifiable claims over time.

openQuestionsAdjacent: What public metrics best proxy 'demonstrated discipline' without gamification?

voiceComparisons.stance: Converges on error correction as central; diverges on institutionalization details.

whatWouldChangeOurMind: A sustained period of public predictions shows calibration materially worse than the internal ledger without corrective updates.
External audit finds systematic mismatch between published claims and internal evidence trails.

openQuestions.summary: When two principles conflict under partial observability, which layer should dominate for firm-tier publication?
openQuestions.unresolvedReason: Meta-analysis flags unresolved tension between severity and closure criteria in this cluster.
openQuestions.layerDisagreementSummary: NLI and judge disagree on whether the contradiction is substantive or linguistic.

openQuestions.summary: How to operationalize 'belief accuracy' for institutions without clean counterfactuals?
openQuestions.unresolvedReason: No agreed measurement protocol yet; competing operationalizations survive adversarial review.
openQuestions.layerDisagreementSummary: Evidence aggregation differs on what counts as a successful prediction.
